front: Play: a woman burns her arm on an iron in this pioneering kitchen sink drama about class pressures
Play: the couple play 'bears and squirrels' upon reuniting
Play: a character jumps up and down shouting 'oooooh!' while making 'paw gestures'
Play: an unseen character named Webster reminds the protagonist of his old love interest Madeline
Play: Alison loses her baby after Jimmy Porter has a falling out with her
Play: Kenneth Tynan wrote a glowing review of this work, contrasting it with country-house dramas he dismissed as 'Loamshire' plays
Play: the title character, a music hall performer, sings 'Thank God I'm normal' and 'Why should I care?'
Play: written at the request of Laurence Olivier, who premiered the title role of Archie Rice
Play: Archie Rice learns his son Mick has been taken as a prisoner of war
Play: ends with Archie Rice walking off stage while music continues to play
Playwright: the Lord Chamberlain banned A Patriot for Me (1965), a play about Alfred Redl, by this author
Playwright: wrote Luther, about the Protestant Reformation
Playwright: created an English-language version of Lope de Vega's A Bond Honored
Playwright: adapted Wilde's The Picture of Dorian Gray for the stage
Playwright: wrote Inadmissible Evidence, featuring the self-hating William Maitland
